content: On the basis of Ellingham diagram, which of the following is not correct?

options.content: Entropy change for all metal oxides is roughly same.
Below the boiling point, T$$\Delta$$S factor is same irrespective of metal.
Above $$\Delta$$G = 0 line, oxide decomposes into metal and oxygen.
If randomness increases the slope increases.

explanation:
$$ - \Delta S = {{\Delta G} \over T}$$, also $${{\Delta G} \over T}$$ is slope in Ellingham diagram. $$\Delta$$S is different for different reactions as they have different slopes.

Rest of the statements (b), (c) and (d) are correct about Ellingham diagram.
